title: The community garden project
explanation:
The community garden project

Two years ago, an empty piece of land behind the library was turned into a community garden. At first, only a few volunteers joined the project, but now more than fifty local residents help there every month. The garden produces vegetables, herbs and flowers, and local schools sometimes bring children to learn about nature. Organisers say the project has improved the area because neighbours now know each other better and spend more time outdoors.

questions.text: The garden was created [*behind the library|next to a school|on a farm].
At first, only [*a few volunteers|fifty residents|schoolchildren] joined.
questions.feedback: Only a few volunteers at first.

questions.text: The garden grows [*vegetables, herbs and flowers|only trees|fruit only].
The project has helped neighbours [*know each other better|move away|work indoors].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The land used to be ____ before the project began. | *empty | busy | private farmland
questions.feedback: It was an empty piece of land.

questions.text: More than fifty residents help there every ____.  | *month | day | year
Schools bring children to learn about ____.  | *nature | law | history
The organisers think the project has improved the ____.  | *area | weather | budget
The text suggests the project has both [*social and environmental benefits|financial losses only|no educational value].
questions.feedback: It mentions community and nature learning.

questions.text: The number of volunteers has [*grown|fallen|stayed the same].
questions.feedback: From a few to over fifty.

questions.text: The garden is an example of a [*local initiative|national law|private company].
The tone of the text is [*positive|critical|uncertain].
